events.title: Ammonia release at Icicle Seafoods plant (Homer, Alaska)
events.description: An ammonia release was reported at the Icicle Seafoods plant in Homer, Alaska. The report noted seven tanks with a total of 43,000 pounds of ammonia, and additional hazardous materials (including chlorine) on scene.

sources.headline: Ammonia release at Icicle Seafoods plant (Homer, Alaska)

key_quotes.quote: An ammonia release occurred at the Icicle Seafoods plant in Homer, Alaska
seven tanks containing ... 43,000 pounds of ammonia

events.title: Fishing vessel F/V Turner Ross sinking with potential diesel release (Pamlico Sound near Wanchese, North Carolina)
events.description: The fishing vessel F/V Turner Ross reportedly sank in shallow water in Pamlico Sound near Wanchese, North Carolina. The owner reported up to about 150 gallons of diesel on board, with vents reportedly plugged and only limited sheening observed.

location.name: Pamlico Sound near Wanchese, North Carolina, USA

sources.headline: Fishing vessel F/V Turner Ross sinking with potential diesel release (Pamlico Sound near Wanchese, North Carolina)

key_quotes.quote: The F/V Turner Ross sank in shallow water
up to ~150 gallons of diesel fuel onboard

events.title: Train derailment involving sodium hydroxide tank cars (Tomkins Cove, New York; Hudson River vicinity)
events.description: A train derailment near Tomkins Cove, New York involved three rail cars carrying sodium hydroxide. One tank car reportedly rolled into the Hudson River and was not believed to be leaking, while another on land was leaking and formed a pool that killed nearby vegetation.

location.name: Tomkins Cove, Rockland County, New York, USA

sources.headline: Train derailment involving sodium hydroxide tank cars (Tomkins Cove, New York; Hudson River vicinity)

key_quotes.quote: Three rail cars carrying sodium hydroxide derailed near the Hudson River
one on land was leaking, forming a pool and killing nearby vegetation

events.title: 2,100-gallon diesel spill from moored motor yacht (Lake Union, Washington)
events.description: A diesel spill was reported on Lake Union in Seattle, Washington after a fuel tank failure led diesel into the bilges and an automatic bilge pump discharged it overboard. The spill volume was reported as approximately 2,100 gallons.

sources.headline: 2,100-gallon diesel spill from moored motor yacht (Lake Union, Washington)

key_quotes.quote: A fuel tank failure ... an automatic bilge pump then discharged the diesel overboard
reported as approximately 2,100 gallons

events.title: Mystery oil slick reported south of Terrebonne Bay/Timbalier Island (Louisiana)
events.description: An oil slick of unknown origin was reported about 3 miles south of Terrebonne Bay/Timbalier Island, Louisiana, described as rainbow sheen and dark brown oil. No responsible party was identified at the time, and cleanup contractors were engaged.

location.name: Offshore waters south of Terrebonne Bay/Timbalier Island, Louisiana, USA

sources.headline: Mystery oil slick reported south of Terrebonne Bay/Timbalier Island (Louisiana)

key_quotes.quote: A slick of unknown origin ... reported approximately 3 miles south of Terrebonne Bay/Timbalier Island
no responsible party was identified ... cleanup contractors were engaged

events.title: Bangladesh monsoon flooding begins in mid-July 1998; FAO notes a peak on 28 July
events.description: FAO situation reporting described heavy monsoon rains beginning in mid-July 1998 that caused extensive flooding across Bangladesh. A later FAO report noted a flood peak on 28 July 1998 during the wider 1998 flood disaster.

events.title: Yangtze River basin flooding episode with early-July flood peaks (around 2–4 July 1998)
events.description: A tutorial/source on the 1998 Yangtze floods describes an early-July period with a flood peak at Yichang on 2 July 1998 and high-water conditions at multiple stations around 4 July 1998 following sustained heavy rainfall. The episode is framed as part of the broader 1998 Yangtze flooding.

location.name: Yangtze River basin, China (referenced via Hubei/Three Gorges area)

sources.headline: tutorial/pd18c

events.title: Levee burst reported in Anxiang County, Hunan during 1998 Yangtze floods (24 July 1998)
events.description: A news archive page reports that a levee burst in Anxiang (Hunan) on 24 July 1998 during the 1998 Yangtze flooding, with large-scale displacement reported. The report highlights levee vulnerability during the flood period.

sources.headline: 199808 news archive

events.title: Kerala monsoon impacts: report cites rain-related death toll reaching 42 (2 July 1998)
events.description: A 2 July 1998 report describes southwest monsoon impacts in Kerala, including flooding and other storm-related damage. The report states that rain-related fatalities had reached 42 in the state, alongside property and crop losses.

sources.headline: 02weath.htm

events.title: NOAA notes exceptional July 1998 heat and rainfall deficits contributing to drought in Texas
events.description: A NOAA Climate Prediction Center special summary describes July 1998 as exceptionally hot across the southern Plains, with Texas experiencing an extremely warm July in the historical record. The summary also notes widespread rainfall deficits contributing to drought conditions in parts of the region.

sources.headline: Special Summaries 98_2

events.title: Offshore northern Papua New Guinea earthquake triggered Aitape-area tsunami
events.description: A strong offshore earthquake near the north coast of Papua New Guinea on 17 July 1998 triggered a destructive tsunami affecting the Aitape/Sissano Lagoon area. Scientific analyses discussed whether submarine landsliding contributed to the tsunami generation.

location.name: Aitape / Sissano Lagoon region, Sandaun Province, Papua New Guinea

sources.headline: Origin of 17 July 1998 Papua New Guinea tsunami: earthquake or landslide?
Papua New Guinea tsunami (1998) information page
Descriptive model: July 17, 1998 Papua New Guinea tsunami

key_quotes.quote: Origin of 17 July 1998 Papua New Guinea tsunami: earthquake or landslide?
Descriptive model: July 17, 1998 Papua New Guinea tsunami

events.title: Faial Island (Azores) earthquake caused casualties and major damage
events.description: A strong earthquake struck the Azores on 9 July 1998, affecting Faial Island and nearby islands, causing fatalities and extensive damage. News reporting described emergency response and significant displacement/homelessness.

sources.headline: UPI Focus: Strong earthquake kills 10 in Azores
Earthquake shakes Azores; at least 10 are feared dead
Simulated ground-motion records for seismic assessment of monumental masonry structures

key_quotes.quote: Strong earthquake kills 10 in Azores
Earthquake shakes Azores; at least 10 are feared dead

events.title: Taiwan Jueili earthquake linked to mapped landslides (inventory dataset)
events.description: A USGS/data.gov record describes an inventory of landslides triggered by the Jueili earthquake in Taiwan on 17 July 1998. The dataset compiles mapped landslide occurrences associated with that earthquake event.

sources.headline: Landslides triggered by Jueili earthquake (dataset record)
Landslides triggered by Jueili earthquake

key_quotes.quote: Landslides triggered by Jueili earthquake

events.title: Flagler County, Florida orders mandatory evacuation amid major wildfires
events.description: Authorities ordered a countywide evacuation in Flagler County, Florida, as multiple wildfires threatened communities and transportation corridors. Reports described large fires that could merge and widespread disruption as residents fled.

sources.headline: Florida wildfires: Flagler County mandatory evacuation (all residents ordered to leave) as multiple fires threatened to merge
Up to 70,000 flee homes as fight against fires falters

key_quotes.quote: Authorities ordered a countywide evacuation in Flagler County
Up to 70,000 flee homes as fight against fires falters

events.title: Florida wildfires disrupt Interstate 95 and prompt evacuations due to smoke and flare-ups
events.description: Reports described renewed wildfire flare-ups in Florida ahead of the July 4 weekend, producing heavy smoke that reduced visibility and disrupted travel, including closures along Interstate 95. The situation led to repeated evacuations in affected areas as dry conditions and new ignitions sustained the fires.

sources.headline: Floridas Relentless Fires Flare Up

key_quotes.quote: Florida's relentless fires flare up

events.title: Prescribed burn near Julian, California escapes containment and burns about 2,100 acres
events.description: A planned prescribed fire near Julian, California escaped containment lines and burned roughly 2,100 acres. Firefighters worked to contain the blaze as it burned away from town, with a report indicating high containment by early July.

sources.headline: Julian-area prescribed burn escaped control near San Diego; about 2,100 acres burned (90% contained by July 3/4 report)

key_quotes.quote: A planned/prescribed fire ... escaped containment lines and burned roughly 2,100 acres

events.title: Neuse River menhaden fish kill with reports of Pfiesteria-like sores
events.description: A fish kill on North Carolina’s Neuse River was reported in late July 1998, with state scientists investigating the cause. Reporting cited large numbers of dead menhaden and described some fish with “Pfiesteria-like” sores, consistent with concerns about harmful algal/dinoflagellate impacts.

sources.headline: NC fish kill 7-29 (WHOI red tide noted events)

key_quotes.quote: approximately 188,000 dead menhaden
“Pfiesteria-like” sores

dedupe.cluster_hint: Neuse River fish kill Pfiesteria-like sores July 1998

events.title: EPA approves site-specific Illinois SIP revision for Riverside Laboratories (Kane County)
events.description: The U.S. EPA issued a direct final rule approving a site-specific revision to the Illinois State Implementation Plan. The action concerns the regulatory status for Riverside Laboratories, Inc. in Kane County under the Clean Air Act SIP framework.

key_quotes.quote: direct final rule approving a site-specific SIP revision submitted by Illinois

events.title: EPA grants Washington final authorization for hazardous waste program revisions (RCRA)
events.description: The U.S. EPA issued an immediate final rule approving Washington State’s request for final authorization of a revision to its hazardous waste program under RCRA. The rule allows Washington to implement the authorized program in lieu of the federal program for specified lands described in the rule, subject to the rule’s effective-date conditions.

key_quotes.quote: immediate final rule approving Washington State’s request for final authorization

events.title: EPA approves Minnesota plan to control landfill gas emissions from existing MSW landfills
events.description: The U.S. EPA issued a direct final rule approving Minnesota’s Clean Air Act section 111(d) state plan implementing the Municipal Solid Waste Landfill Emission Guidelines. The action makes standards to control landfill gas emissions from existing MSW landfills federally enforceable in Minnesota.

key_quotes.quote: approving Minnesota’s Clean Air Act section 111(d) state plan

events.title: EPA updates Superfund National Priorities List by adding nine sites
events.description: The U.S. EPA issued a final rule revising Appendix B to the National Oil and Hazardous Substances Pollution Contingency Plan by adding nine sites to the National Priorities List. The listing helps prioritize sites for further investigation and potential remedial action under the Superfund program.

location.name: Washington, District of Columbia, United States

key_quotes.quote: final rule revising Appendix B ... by adding nine sites to the National Priorities List
